title: Agile Product Lifecycle

items.label: Ideation & Discovery
items.description: Generate and validate product ideas by understanding user needs and market opportunities through research and brainstorming.
items.imagePrompt: A glowing lightbulb surrounded by diverse people sharing ideas in a creative workspace with sticky notes and sketches.

items.label: Planning & Prioritization
items.description: Define product vision, roadmap, and prioritize features based on impact, feasibility, and user feedback to guide development focus.
items.imagePrompt: A team gathered around a table with charts, a roadmap on a whiteboard, and sticky notes representing prioritized tasks.

items.label: Development & Iteration
items.description: Build product increments in short cycles, continuously integrating feedback and improving features through agile sprints.
items.imagePrompt: Developers collaborating at computers with code on screens, sprint boards, and iterative feedback loops visible.

items.label: Testing & Validation
items.description: Conduct rigorous testing including user acceptance and quality assurance to ensure product meets requirements and delight users.
items.imagePrompt: Testers examining software on devices, checking for bugs, and users providing feedback in a lab-like environment.

items.label: Launch & Scale
items.description: Release the product to market, monitor performance, and scale operations while iterating based on user data and market trends.
items.imagePrompt: A rocket launching from a platform with graphs and user engagement icons symbolizing growth and scaling.

contentBlocks.text: The Agile Product Lifecycle is a continuous, iterative process that fosters rapid learning and adaptation to deliver customer value effectively.
contentBlocks.label: Key Insight

imagePrompt: A dynamic, collaborative workspace filled with diverse team members actively engaging in creative discussions and digital tools, radiating energy and innovation.
